Overview

Address
1200 FIRST AVENUE NORTHEAST, LITTLE FALLS, MN 56345
Phone
3206322061
Certified beds
64
Avg daily residents
53 (83% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit (other)
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1986-12-01
Setting
Rural
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.82 3.23 4.56 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.80 0.57 0.95 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.24 0.72 0.29 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.78 1.95 3.32 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.04 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.05 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.26
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.41
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.89
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
46.0%
annual
RN turnover
33.3%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.15
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.83
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-04-18 15 10 7 68 1 68
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-06-29 24 7 17 100 1 100
Total weighted health score
76.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (42)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

3 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
38 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-12-31 2025-12-22
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-08-22 2025-09-22
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-08-22 2025-09-22
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2025-08-22 2025-09-22
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2025-08-07 2025-09-09
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-02-04 2025-02-27
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-08-23 2024-09-18
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-20
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-24
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-24
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-24
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-24
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-24
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-24
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. C 2024-04-18 2024-05-24
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-24
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-04-18 2024-05-24
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. C 2024-04-18 2024-05-24
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-03-06 2024-04-08
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-03-06 2024-04-08
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-03-06 2024-04-08
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-03-06 2024-04-08
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-03-06 2024-04-08
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-01-25 2024-03-03
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-01-25 2024-03-03
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-01-25 2024-03-03
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-01-25 2024-03-03
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2023-09-14 2023-10-27
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2023-09-14 2023-10-27
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2023-06-29 2023-08-28
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2023-06-29 2023-08-28
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. E 2023-06-29 2023-08-28
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2023-06-29 2023-08-28
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2023-06-29 2023-08-28
0825 Provide or get specialized rehabilitative services as required for a resident. D 2023-06-29 2023-08-28
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. E 2023-06-29 2023-08-28
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2023-04-04 2023-05-09
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2023-04-04 2023-05-09
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2023-03-31 2023-05-05
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2022-06-30 2022-08-03
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2022-06-30 2022-08-03
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2022-06-30 2022-08-03

Deficiencies 0758 — correction_date 2024-03-03 0758.correction_date
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — scope_severity D 0758.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0758 — scope_severity D 0758.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0758 — survey_date 2024-04-18 0758.survey_date

Deficiencies 0758 — tag 0758 0758.tag
Deficiencies 0758 — tag 0758 0758.tag
Deficiencies 0761 — correction_date 2022-08-03 0761.correction_date
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — scope_severity D 0761.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0761 — survey_date 2022-06-30 0761.survey_date
Deficiencies 0761 — tag 0761 0761.tag

Deficiencies 0791 — description Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. 0791.description

Deficiencies 0791 — survey_date 2023-09-14 0791.survey_date
Deficiencies 0791 — tag 0791 0791.tag

Deficiencies 0804 — description Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. 0804.description
Deficiencies 0804 — scope_severity D 0804.scope_severity
